    21→
    22→// ApplyOpts are options that affect the details of how Terraform will apply a
    23→// previously-generated plan.
    24→type ApplyOpts struct {
    25→	// ExternalProviders is a set of pre-configured provider instances with
    26→	// the same purpose as [PlanOpts.ExternalProviders].
    27→	//
    28→	// Callers must pass providers that are configured in a similar way as
    29→	// the providers that were passed when creating the plan that's being
    30→	// applied, or the results will be erratic.
    31→	ExternalProviders map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
    32→
    33→	// SetVariables are the raw values for root module variables as provided
    34→	// by the user who is requesting the run, prior to any normalization or
    35→	// substitution of defaults. See the documentation for the InputValue
    36→	// type for more information on how to correctly populate this.
    37→	//
    38→	// During the apply phase it's only valid to specify values for input
    39→	// values that were declared as ephemeral, because all other input
    40→	// values must retain the values that were specified during planning.
    41→	SetVariables InputValues
    42→}
    43→
    44→// ApplyOpts creates an [ApplyOpts] with copies of all of the elements that
    45→// are expected to propagate from plan to apply when planning and applying
    46→// in the same process.
    47→//
    48→// In practice planning and applying are often separated into two different
    49→// executions, in which case callers must retain enough information between
    50→// plan and apply to construct an equivalent [ApplyOpts] themselves without
    51→// using this function. This is here mainly for convenient internal use such
    52→// as in test cases.
    53→func (po *PlanOpts) ApplyOpts() *ApplyOpts {
    54→	return &ApplyOpts{
    55→		ExternalProviders: po.ExternalProviders,
    56→	}
    57→}
    58→
    59→// Apply performs the actions described by the given Plan object and returns
    60→// the resulting updated state.
    61→//
    62→// The given configuration *must* be the same configuration that was passed
    63→// earlier to Context.Plan in order to create this plan.
    64→//
    65→// Even if the returned diagnostics contains errors, Apply always returns the
    66→// resulting state which is likely to have been partially-updated.
    67→//
    68→// The [opts] argument may be nil to indicate that no special options are
    69→// required. In that case, Apply will use a default set of options. Some
    70→// options in [PlanOpts] when creating a plan must be echoed with equivalent
    71→// settings during apply, so leaving opts as nil might not be valid for
    72→// certain combinations of plan-time options.
    73→func (c *Context) Apply(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *ApplyOpts) (*states.State, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    74→	state, _, diags := c.ApplyAndEval(plan, config, opts)
    75→	return state, diags
    76→}
    77→
    78→// ApplyAndEval is like [Context.Apply] except that it additionally makes a
    79→// best effort to return a [lang.Scope] which can evaluate expressions in the
    80→// root module based on the content of the new state.
    81→//
    82→// The scope will be nil if the apply process doesn't complete successfully
    83→// enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned state is nil
    84→// then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope
    85→// to be nil even when the state isn't, if the apply didn't complete enough for
    86→// the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.
    87→func (c *Context) ApplyAndEval(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *ApplyOpts) (*states.State, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    88→	defer c.acquireRun("apply")()
    89→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
    90→
    91→	if plan == nil {
    92→		panic("cannot apply nil plan")
    93→	}
    94→	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Building and walking apply graph for %s plan", plan.UIMode)
    95→
    96→	if opts == nil {
    97→		opts = &ApplyOpts{}
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	if plan.Errored {
   101→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   102→			tfdiags.Error,
   103→			"Cannot apply failed plan",
   104→			`The given plan is incomplete due to errors during planning, and so it cannot be applied.`,
   105→		))
   106→		return nil, nil, diags
   107→	}
   108→	if !plan.Applyable {
   109→		if plan.Changes.Empty() {
   110→			// If a plan is not applyable but it didn't have any errors then that
   111→			// suggests it was a "no-op" plan, which doesn't really do any
   112→			// harm to apply, so we'll just do it but leave ourselves a note
   113→			// in the trace log in case it ends up relevant to a bug report.
   114→			log.Printf("[TRACE] Applying a no-op plan")
   115→		} else {
   116→			// This situation isn't something we expect, since our own rules
   117→			// for what "applyable" means make this scenario impossible. We'll
   118→			// reject it on the assumption that something very strange is
   119→			// going on. and so better to halt than do something incorrect.
   120→			// This error message is generic and useless because we don't
   121→			// expect anyone to ever see it in normal use.
   122→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   123→				tfdiags.Error,
   124→				"Cannot apply non-applyable plan",
   125→				`The given plan is not applyable. If this seems like a bug in Terraform, then please report it!`,
   126→			))
   127→			return nil, nil, diags
   128→		}
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	// The caller must provide values for all of the "apply-time variables"
   132→	// mentioned in the plan, and for no others because the others come from
   133→	// the plan itself.
   134→	// Apply-time variable check handled by graph walk validation.
   135→
   136→	for _, rc := range plan.Changes.Resources {
   137→		// Import is a no-op change during an apply (all the real action happens during the plan) but we'd
   138→		// like to show some helpful output that mirrors the way we show other changes.
   139→		if rc.Importing != nil {
   140→			hookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{
   141→				Addr:         rc.Addr,
   142→				ProviderAddr: rc.ProviderAddr.Provider,
   143→			}
   144→			for _, h := range c.hooks {
   145→				// In future, we may need to call PostApplyImport separately elsewhere in the apply
   146→				// operation. For now, though, we'll call Pre and Post hooks together.
   147→				h.PreApplyImport(hookResourceID, *rc.Importing)
   148→				h.PostApplyImport(hookResourceID, *rc.Importing)
   149→			}
   150→		}
   151→	}
   152→
   153→	graph, operation, moreDiags := c.applyGraph(plan, config, opts, true)
   154→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   155→	if moreDiags.HasErrors() {
   156→		return nil, nil, diags
   157→	}
   158→
   159→	moreDiags = checkExternalProviders(config, plan, nil, opts.ExternalProviders)
   160→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   161→	if moreDiags.HasErrors() {
   162→		return nil, nil, diags
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, plan.PriorState)
   166→	diags = diags.Append(schemaDiags)
   167→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   168→		return nil, nil, diags
   169→	}
   170→
   171→	changes, err := plan.Changes.Decode(schemas)
   172→	if err != nil {
   173→		diags = diags.Append(err)
   174→		return nil, nil, diags
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	workingState := plan.PriorState.DeepCopy()
   178→	walker, walkDiags := c.walk(graph, operation, &graphWalkOpts{
   179→		Config:                  config,
   180→		InputState:              workingState,
   181→		Changes:                 changes,
   182→		Overrides:               plan.Overrides,
   183→		ExternalProviderConfigs: opts.ExternalProviders,
   184→
   185→		DeferralAllowed: true,
   186→
   187→		// We need to propagate the check results from the plan phase,
   188→		// because that will tell us which checkable objects we're expecting
   189→		// to see updated results from during the apply step.
   190→		PlanTimeCheckResults: plan.Checks,
   191→
   192→		// We also want to propagate the timestamp from the plan file.
   193→		PlanTimeTimestamp: plan.Timestamp,
   194→
   195→		ProviderFuncResults: providers.NewFunctionResultsTable(plan.ProviderFunctionResults),
   196→	})
   197→	diags = diags.Append(walker.NonFatalDiagnostics)
   198→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   199→
   200→	// After the walk is finished, we capture a simplified snapshot of the
   201→	// check result data as part of the new state.
   202→	walker.State.RecordCheckResults(walker.Checks)
   203→
   204→	newState := walker.State.Close()
   205→	if plan.UIMode == plans.DestroyMode && !diags.HasErrors() {
   206→		// NOTE: This is a vestigial violation of the rule that we mustn't
   207→		// use plan.UIMode to affect apply-time behavior.
   208→		// We ideally ought to just call newState.PruneResourceHusks
   209→		// unconditionally here, but we historically didn't and haven't yet
   210→		// verified that it'd be safe to do so.
   211→		newState.PruneResourceHusks()
   212→	}
   213→
   214→	if len(plan.TargetAddrs) > 0 {
   215→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   216→			tfdiags.Warning,
   217→			"Applied changes may be incomplete",
   218→			`The plan was created with the -target option in effect, so some changes requested in the configuration may have been ignored and the output values may not be fully updated. Run the following command to verify that no other changes are pending:
   219→    terraform plan
   220→
   221→Note that the -target option is not suitable for routine use, and is provided only for exceptional situations such as recovering from errors or mistakes, or when Terraform specifically suggests to use it as part of an error message.`,
   222→		))
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	// FIXME: we cannot check for an empty plan for refresh-only, because root
   226→	// outputs are always stored as changes. The final condition of the state
   227→	// also depends on some cleanup which happens during the apply walk. It
   228→	// would probably make more sense if applying a refresh-only plan were
   229→	// simply just returning the planned state and checks, but some extra
   230→	// cleanup is going to be needed to make the plan state match what apply
   231→	// would do. For now we can copy the checks over which were overwritten
   232→	// during the apply walk.
   233→	// Despite the intent of UIMode, it must still be used for apply-time
   234→	// differences in destroy plans too, so we can make use of that here as
   235→	// well.
   236→	if plan.UIMode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   237→		newState.CheckResults = plan.Checks.DeepCopy()
   238→	}
   239→
   240→	// The caller also gets access to an expression evaluation scope in the
   241→	// root module, in case it needs to extract other information using
   242→	// expressions, like in "terraform console" or the test harness.
   243→	evalScope := evalScopeFromGraphWalk(walker, addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   244→
   245→	return newState, evalScope, diags
   246→}
   247→
   248→func checkApplyTimeVariables(needed collections.Set[string], gotValues InputValues, config *configs.Config) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   249→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   250→	for name := range needed.All() {
   251→		if vv, exists := gotValues[name]; !exists || vv.Value == cty.NilVal || vv.Value.IsNull() {
   252→			// This error message assumes that the only possible reason for
   253→			// an apply-time variable is because the variable is ephemeral,
   254→			// which is true at the time of writing. This error message might
   255→			// need to be generalized if we introduce other reasons for
   256→			// apply-time variables in future.
   257→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   258→				tfdiags.Error,
   259→				"No value for required variable",
   260→				fmt.Sprintf("The ephemeral input variable %q was set during the plan phase, and so must also be set during the apply phase.", name),
   261→			))
   262→		}
   263→	}
   264→	for name := range gotValues {
   265→		if !needed.Has(name) {
   266→			// We'll treat this a little differently depending on whether
   267→			// the variable is declared as ephemeral or not.
   268→			if vc, ok := config.Module.Variables[name]; ok && vc.Ephemeral {
   269→				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   270→					tfdiags.Error,
   271→					"No value for required variable",
   272→					fmt.Sprintf("The ephemeral input variable %q was not set during the plan phase, and so must remain unset during the apply phase.", name),
   273→				))
   274→			} else {
   275→				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   276→					tfdiags.Error,
   277→					"Unexpected new value for variable",
   278→					fmt.Sprintf("Input variable %q is non-ephemeral, so its value was fixed during planning and cannot be reset during apply.", name),
   279→				))
   280→			}
   281→		}
   282→	}
   283→	return diags
   284→}
   285→
   286→func (c *Context) applyGraph(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *ApplyOpts, validate bool) (*Graph, walkOperation, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   287→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   288→
   289→	variables := InputValues{}
   290→	for name, dyVal := range plan.VariableValues {
   291→		val, err := dyVal.Decode(cty.DynamicPseudoType)
   292→		if err != nil {
   293→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   294→				tfdiags.Error,
   295→				"Invalid variable value in plan",
   296→				fmt.Sprintf("Invalid value for variable %q recorded in plan file: %s.", name, err),
   297→			))
   298→			continue
   299→		}
   300→		if pvm, ok := plan.VariableMarks[name]; ok {
   301→			val = val.MarkWithPaths(pvm)
   302→		}
   303→
   304→		variables[name] = &InputValue{
   305→			Value:      val,
   306→			SourceType: ValueFromPlan,
   307→		}
   308→	}
   309→	// Apply-time variables need to be merged in too.
   310→	// FIXME: We should check that all of these match declared variables and
   311→	// that all of them are declared as ephemeral, because all non-ephemeral
   312→	// variables are supposed to come exclusively from plan.VariableValues.
   313→	if opts != nil {
   314→		for n, vv := range opts.SetVariables {
   315→			variables[n] = vv
   316→		}
   317→	}
   318→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   319→		return nil, walkApply, diags
   320→	}
   321→
   322→	// The plan.VariableValues field only records variables that were actually
   323→	// set by the caller in the PlanOpts, so we may need to provide
   324→	// placeholders for any other variables that the user didn't set, in
   325→	// which case Terraform will once again use the default value from the
   326→	// configuration when we visit these variables during the graph walk.
   327→	for name := range config.Module.Variables {
   328→		if _, ok := variables[name]; ok {
   329→			continue
   330→		}
   331→		variables[name] = &InputValue{
   332→			Value:      cty.NilVal,
   333→			SourceType: ValueFromPlan,
   334→		}
   335→	}
   336→
   337→	operation := walkApply
   338→	if plan.UIMode == plans.DestroyMode {
   339→		// FIXME: Due to differences in how objects must be handled in the
   340→		// graph and evaluated during a complete destroy, we must continue to
   341→		// use plans.DestroyMode to switch on this behavior. If all objects
   342→		// which require special destroy handling can be tracked in the plan,
   343→		// then this switch will no longer be needed and we can remove the
   344→		// walkDestroy operation mode.
   345→		// TODO: Audit that and remove walkDestroy as an operation mode.
   346→		operation = walkDestroy
   347→	}
   348→
   349→	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   350→	if opts != nil {
   351→		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   352→	}
   353→
   354→	graph, moreDiags := (&ApplyGraphBuilder{
   355→		Config:                  config,
   356→		Changes:                 plan.Changes,
   357→		DeferredChanges:         plan.DeferredResources,
   358→		State:                   plan.PriorState,
   359→		RootVariableValues:      variables,
   360→		ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   361→		Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   362→		Targets:                 plan.TargetAddrs,
   363→		ForceReplace:            plan.ForceReplaceAddrs,
   364→		Operation:               operation,
   365→		ExternalReferences:      plan.ExternalReferences,
   366→		Overrides:               plan.Overrides,
   367→	}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   368→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   369→	if moreDiags.HasErrors() {
   370→		return nil, walkApply, diags
   371→	}
   372→
   373→	return graph, operation, diags
   374→}
   375→
   376→// ApplyGraphForUI is a last vestage of graphs in the public interface of
   377→// Context (as opposed to graphs as an implementation detail) intended only for
   378→// use by the "terraform graph" command when asked to render an apply-time
   379→// graph.
   380→//
   381→// The result of this is intended only for rendering ot the user as a dot
   382→// graph, and so may change in future in order to make the result more useful
   383→// in that context, even if drifts away from the physical graph that Terraform
   384→// Core currently uses as an implementation detail of applying.
   385→func (c *Context) ApplyGraphForUI(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   386→	// For now though, this really is just the internal graph, confusing
   387→	// implementation details and all.
   388→
   389→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   390→
   391→	graph, _, moreDiags := c.applyGraph(plan, config, nil, false)
   392→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   393→	return graph, diags
   394→}
   395→

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// HookAction is an enum of actions that can be taken as a result of a hook
    16→// callback. This allows you to modify the behavior of Terraform at runtime.
    17→type HookAction byte
    18→
    19→const (
    20→	// HookActionContinue continues with processing as usual.
    21→	HookActionContinue HookAction = iota
    22→
    23→	// HookActionHalt halts immediately: no more hooks are processed
    24→	// and the action that Terraform was about to take is cancelled.
    25→	HookActionHalt
    26→)
    27→
    28→// HookResourceIdentity is passed to Hook interface methods to fully identify
    29→// the resource instance being operated on. It currently includes the resource
    30→// address and the provider address.
    31→type HookResourceIdentity struct {
    32→	Addr         addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    33→	ProviderAddr addrs.Provider
    34→}
    35→
    36→// Hook is the interface that must be implemented to hook into various
    37→// parts of Terraform, allowing you to inspect or change behavior at runtime.
    38→//
    39→// There are MANY hook points into Terraform. If you only want to implement
    40→// some hook points, but not all (which is the likely case), then embed the
    41→// NilHook into your struct, which implements all of the interface but does
    42→// nothing. Then, override only the functions you want to implement.
    43→type Hook interface {
    44→	// PreApply and PostApply are called before and after an action for a
    45→	// single instance is applied. The error argument in PostApply is the
    46→	// error, if any, that was returned from the provider Apply call itself.
    47→	PreApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    48→	PostApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, newState cty.Value, err error) (HookAction, error)
    49→
    50→	// PreDiff and PostDiff are called before and after a provider is given
    51→	// the opportunity to customize the proposed new state to produce the
    52→	// planned new state.
    53→	PreDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState, proposedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    54→	PostDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    55→
    56→	// The provisioning hooks signal both the overall start end end of
    57→	// provisioning for a particular instance and of each of the individual
    58→	// configured provisioners for each instance. The sequence of these
    59→	// for a given instance might look something like this:
    60→	//
    61→	//          PreProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    62→	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file")
    63→	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file", nil)
    64→	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec")
    65→	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Installing foo...")
    66→	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Configuring bar...")
    67→	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", nil)
    68→	//         PostProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    69→	//
    70→	// ProvisionOutput is called with output sent back by the provisioners.
    71→	// This will be called multiple times as output comes in, with each call
    72→	// representing one line of output. It cannot control whether the
    73→	// provisioner continues running.
    74→	PreProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    75→	PostProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    76→	PreProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string) (HookAction, error)
    77→	PostProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, err error) (HookAction, error)
    78→	ProvisionOutput(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, line string)
    79→
    80→	// PreRefresh and PostRefresh are called before and after a single
    81→	// resource state is refreshed, respectively.
    82→	PreRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    83→	PostRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value, newState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    84→
    85→	// PreImportState and PostImportState are called before and after
    86→	// (respectively) each state import operation for a given resource address when
    87→	// using the legacy import command.
    88→	PreImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error)
    89→	PostImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error)
    90→
    91→	// PrePlanImport and PostPlanImport are called during a plan before and after planning to import
    92→	// a new resource using the configuration-driven import workflow.
    93→	PrePlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error)
    94→	PostPlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error)
    95→
    96→	// PreApplyImport and PostApplyImport are called during an apply for each imported resource when
    97→	// using the configuration-driven import workflow.
    98→	PreApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error)
    99→	PostApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error)
   100→
   101→	// PreEphemeralOp and PostEphemeralOp are called during an operation on ephemeral resource
   102→	// such as opening, renewal or closing
   103→	PreEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action) (HookAction, error)
   104→	PostEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action, opErr error) (HookAction, error)
   105→
   106→	// Stopping is called if an external signal requests that Terraform
   107→	// gracefully abort an operation in progress.
   108→	//
   109→	// This notification might suggest that the user wants Terraform to exit
   110→	// ASAP and in that case it's possible that if Terraform runs for too much
   111→	// longer then it'll get killed un-gracefully, and so this hook could be
   112→	// an opportunity to persist any transient data that would be lost under
   113→	// a subsequent kill signal. However, implementations must take care to do
   114→	// so in a way that won't cause corruption if the process _is_ killed while
   115→	// this hook is still running.
   116→	//
   117→	// This hook cannot control whether Terraform continues, because the
   118→	// graceful shutdown process is typically already running by the time this
   119→	// function is called.
   120→	Stopping()
   121→
   122→	// PostStateUpdate is called each time the state is updated. The caller must
   123→	// coordinate a lock for the state if necessary, such that the Hook may
   124→	// access it freely without any need for additional locks to protect from
   125→	// concurrent writes. Implementations which modify or retain the state after
   126→	// the call has returned must copy the state.
   127→	PostStateUpdate(new *states.State) (HookAction, error)
   128→}
   129→
   130→// NilHook is a Hook implementation that does nothing. It exists only to
   131→// simplify implementing hooks. You can embed this into your Hook implementation
   132→// and only implement the functions you are interested in.
   133→type NilHook struct{}
   134→
   135→var _ Hook = (*NilHook)(nil)
   136→
   137→func (*NilHook) PreApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   138→	return HookActionHalt, nil
   139→}
   140→
   141→func (*NilHook) PostApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, newState cty.Value, err error) (HookAction, error) {
   142→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   143→}
   144→
   145→func (*NilHook) PreDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState, proposedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   146→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   147→}
   148→
   149→func (*NilHook) PostDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   150→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   151→}
   152→
   153→func (*NilHook) PreProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   154→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   155→}
   156→
   157→func (*NilHook) PostProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   158→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   159→}
   160→
   161→func (*NilHook) PreProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string) (HookAction, error) {
   162→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   163→}
   164→
   165→func (*NilHook) PostProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, err error) (HookAction, error) {
   166→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   167→}
   168→
   169→func (*NilHook) ProvisionOutput(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, line string) {
   170→}
   171→
   172→func (*NilHook) PreRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   173→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   174→}
   175→
   176→func (*NilHook) PostRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value, newState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   177→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   178→}
   179→
   180→func (*NilHook) PreImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error) {
   181→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   182→}
   183→
   184→func (*NilHook) PostImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error) {
   185→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   186→}
   187→
   188→func (h *NilHook) PrePlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error) {
   189→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   190→}
   191→
   192→func (h *NilHook) PostPlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error) {
   193→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   194→}
   195→
   196→func (h *NilHook) PreApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error) {
   197→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   198→}
   199→
   200→func (h *NilHook) PostApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error) {
   201→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   202→}
   203→
   204→func (h *NilHook) PreEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action) (HookAction, error) {
   205→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   206→}
   207→
   208→func (h *NilHook) PostEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action, opErr error) (HookAction, error) {
   209→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   210→}
   211→
   212→func (*NilHook) Stopping() {
   213→	// Does nothing at all by default
   214→}
   215→
   216→func (*NilHook) PostStateUpdate(new *states.State) (HookAction, error) {
   217→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   218→}

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"

    15→// HookAction is an enum of actions that can be taken as a result of a hook
    16→// callback. This allows you to modify the behavior of Terraform at runtime.
    17→type HookAction byte
    18→
    19→const (
    20→	// HookActionContinue continues with processing as usual.
    21→	HookActionContinue HookAction = iota
    22→
    23→	// HookActionHalt halts immediately: no more hooks are processed
    24→	// and the action that Terraform was about to take is cancelled.
    25→	HookActionHalt
    26→)
    27→
    28→// HookResourceIdentity is passed to Hook interface methods to fully identify
    29→// the resource instance being operated on. It currently includes the resource
    30→// address and the provider address.
    31→type HookResourceIdentity struct {
    32→	Addr         addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    33→	ProviderAddr addrs.Provider
    34→}
    35→
    36→// Hook is the interface that must be implemented to hook into various
    37→// parts of Terraform, allowing you to inspect or change behavior at runtime.
    38→//
    39→// There are MANY hook points into Terraform. If you only want to implement
    40→// some hook points, but not all (which is the likely case), then embed the
    41→// NilHook into your struct, which implements all of the interface but does
    42→// nothing. Then, override only the functions you want to implement.
    43→type Hook interface {
    44→	// PreApply and PostApply are called before and after an action for a
    45→	// single instance is applied. The error argument in PostApply is the
    46→	// error, if any, that was returned from the provider Apply call itself.
    47→	PreApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    48→	PostApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, newState cty.Value, err error) (HookAction, error)
    49→
    50→	// PreDiff and PostDiff are called before and after a provider is given
    51→	// the opportunity to customize the proposed new state to produce the
    52→	// planned new state.
    53→	PreDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState, proposedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    54→	PostDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    55→
    56→	// The provisioning hooks signal both the overall start end end of
    57→	// provisioning for a particular instance and of each of the individual
    58→	// configured provisioners for each instance. The sequence of these
    59→	// for a given instance might look something like this:
    60→	//
    61→	//          PreProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    62→	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file")
    63→	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file", nil)
    64→	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec")
    65→	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Installing foo...")
    66→	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Configuring bar...")
    67→	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", nil)
    68→	//         PostProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    69→	//
    70→	// ProvisionOutput is called with output sent back by the provisioners.
    71→	// This will be called multiple times as output comes in, with each call
    72→	// representing one line of output. It cannot control whether the
    73→	// provisioner continues running.
    74→	PreProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    75→	PostProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    76→	PreProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string) (HookAction, error)
    77→	PostProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, err error) (HookAction, error)
    78→	ProvisionOutput(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, line string)
    79→
    80→	// PreRefresh and PostRefresh are called before and after a single
    81→	// resource state is refreshed, respectively.
    82→	PreRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    83→	PostRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value, newState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    84→
    85→	// PreImportState and PostImportState are called before and after
    86→	// (respectively) each state import operation for a given resource address when
    87→	// using the legacy import command.
    88→	PreImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error)
    89→	PostImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error)
    90→
    91→	// PrePlanImport and PostPlanImport are called during a plan before and after planning to import
    92→	// a new resource using the configuration-driven import workflow.
    93→	PrePlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error)
    94→	PostPlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error)
    95→
    96→	// PreApplyImport and PostApplyImport are called during an apply for each imported resource when
    97→	// using the configuration-driven import workflow.
    98→	PreApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error)
    99→	PostApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error)
   100→
   101→	// PreEphemeralOp and PostEphemeralOp are called during an operation on ephemeral resource
   102→	// such as opening, renewal or closing
   103→	PreEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action) (HookAction, error)
   104→	PostEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action, opErr error) (HookAction, error)
   105→
   106→	// Stopping is called if an external signal requests that Terraform
   107→	// gracefully abort an operation in progress.
   108→	//
   109→	// This notification might suggest that the user wants Terraform to exit
   110→	// ASAP and in that case it's possible that if Terraform runs for too much
   111→	// longer then it'll get killed un-gracefully, and so this hook could be
   112→	// an opportunity to persist any transient data that would be lost under
   113→	// a subsequent kill signal. However, implementations must take care to do
   114→	// so in a way that won't cause corruption if the process _is_ killed while
   115→	// this hook is still running.
   116→	//
   117→	// This hook cannot control whether Terraform continues, because the
   118→	// graceful shutdown process is typically already running by the time this
   119→	// function is called.
   120→	Stopping()
   121→
   122→	// PostStateUpdate is called each time the state is updated. The caller must
   123→	// coordinate a lock for the state if necessary, such that the Hook may
   124→	// access it freely without any need for additional locks to protect from
   125→	// concurrent writes. Implementations which modify or retain the state after
   126→	// the call has returned must copy the state.
   127→	PostStateUpdate(new *states.State) (HookAction, error)
   128→}

   130→// NilHook is a Hook implementation that does nothing. It exists only to
   131→// simplify implementing hooks. You can embed this into your Hook implementation
   132→// and only implement the functions you are interested in.
   133→type NilHook struct{}
   134→
   135→var _ Hook = (*NilHook)(nil)
   136→
   137→func (*NilHook) PreApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   138→	return HookActionHalt, nil
   139→}

   141→func (*NilHook) PostApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, newState cty.Value, err error) (HookAction, error) {
   142→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   143→}

   145→func (*NilHook) PreDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState, proposedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   146→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   147→}

   150→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   151→}

   154→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   155→}
   156→
   157→func (*NilHook) PostProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   158→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   159→}
   160→
   161→func (*NilHook) PreProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string) (HookAction, error) {
   162→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   163→}
   164→
   165→func (*NilHook) PostProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, err error) (HookAction, error) {
   166→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   167→}
   168→
   169→func (*NilHook) ProvisionOutput(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, line string) {
   170→}
   171→
   172→func (*NilHook) PreRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   173→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   174→}
   175→
   176→func (*NilHook) PostRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value, newState cty.Value) (HookAction, error) {
   177→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   178→}
   179→
   180→func (*NilHook) PreImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error) {
   181→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   182→}
   183→
   184→func (*NilHook) PostImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error) {
   185→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   186→}
   187→
   188→func (h *NilHook) PrePlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error) {
   189→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   190→}
   191→
   192→func (h *NilHook) PostPlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error) {
   193→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   194→}
   195→
   196→func (h *NilHook) PreApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error) {
   197→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   198→}
   199→
   200→func (h *NilHook) PostApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error) {
   201→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   202→}
   203→
   204→func (h *NilHook) PreEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action) (HookAction, error) {
   205→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   206→}
   207→
   208→func (h *NilHook) PostEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action, opErr error) (HookAction, error) {
   209→	return HookActionContinue, nil
   210→}
   211→
   212→func (*NilHook) Stopping() {
   213→	// Does nothing at all by default
   214→}

   217→	return HookActionContinue, nil

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"

    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"

    24→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/resources/ephemeral"
