    26→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    27→)
    28→
    29→// Evaluator provides the necessary contextual data for evaluating expressions
    30→// for a particular walk operation.
    31→type Evaluator struct {
    32→	// Operation defines what type of operation this evaluator is being used
    33→	// for.
    34→	Operation walkOperation
    35→
    36→	// Meta is contextual metadata about the current operation.
    37→	Meta *ContextMeta
    38→
    39→	// Config is the root node in the configuration tree.
    40→	Config *configs.Config
    41→
    42→	// Instances tracks the dynamic instances that are associated with each
    43→	// module call or resource. The graph walk gradually registers the
    44→	// set of instances for each object within the graph nodes for those
    45→	// objects, and so as long as the graph has been built correctly the
    46→	// set of instances for an object should always be available by the time
    47→	// we're evaluating expressions that refer to it.
    48→	Instances *instances.Expander
    49→
    50→	// NamedValues is where we keep the values of already-evaluated input
    51→	// variables, local values, and output values.
    52→	NamedValues *namedvals.State
    53→
    54→	// EphemeralResources tracks the currently-open instances of any ephemeral
    55→	// resources.
    56→	EphemeralResources *ephemeral.Resources
    57→
    58→	// Deferrals tracks resources and modules that have had either their
    59→	// expansion or their specific planned actions deferred to a future
    60→	// plan/apply round.
    61→	Deferrals *deferring.Deferred
    62→
    63→	// Plugins is the library of available plugin components (providers and
    64→	// provisioners) that we have available to help us evaluate expressions
    65→	// that interact with plugin-provided objects.
    66→	//
    67→	// From this we only access the schemas of the plugins, and don't otherwise
    68→	// interact with plugin instances.
    69→	Plugins *contextPlugins
    70→
    71→	// State is the current state, embedded in a wrapper that ensures that
    72→	// it can be safely accessed and modified concurrently.
    73→	State *states.SyncState
    74→
    75→	// Changes is the set of proposed changes, embedded in a wrapper that
    76→	// ensures they can be safely accessed and modified concurrently.
    77→	Changes *plans.ChangesSync
    78→
    79→	PlanTimestamp time.Time
    80→}
    81→
    82→// Scope creates an evaluation scope for the given module path and optional
    83→// resource.
    84→//
    85→// If the "self" argument is nil then the "self" object is not available
    86→// in evaluated expressions. Otherwise, it behaves as an alias for the given
    87→// address.
    88→func (e *Evaluator) Scope(data lang.Data, self addrs.Referenceable, source addrs.Referenceable, extFuncs lang.ExternalFuncs) *lang.Scope {
    89→	return &lang.Scope{
    90→		Data:          data,
    91→		ParseRef:      addrs.ParseRef,
    92→		SelfAddr:      self,
    93→		SourceAddr:    source,
    94→		PureOnly:      e.Operation != walkApply && e.Operation != walkDestroy && e.Operation != walkEval,
    95→		BaseDir:       ".", // Always current working directory for now.
    96→		PlanTimestamp: e.PlanTimestamp,
    97→		ExternalFuncs: extFuncs,
    98→	}
    99→}
   100→
   101→// evaluationStateData is an implementation of lang.Data that resolves
   102→// references primarily (but not exclusively) using information from a State.
   103→type evaluationStateData struct {
   104→	*evaluationData
   105→
   106→	// ModulePath is the path through the dynamic module tree to the module
   107→	// that references will be resolved relative to.
   108→	ModulePath addrs.ModuleInstance
   109→
   110→	// InstanceKeyData describes the values, if any, that are accessible due
   111→	// to repetition of a containing object using "count" or "for_each"
   112→	// arguments. (It is _not_ used for the for_each inside "dynamic" blocks,
   113→	// since the user specifies in that case which variable name to locally
   114→	// shadow.)
   115→	InstanceKeyData InstanceKeyEvalData
   116→
   117→	// Operation records the type of walk the evaluationStateData is being used
   118→	// for.
   119→	Operation walkOperation
   120→}
   121→
   122→// InstanceKeyEvalData is the old name for instances.RepetitionData, aliased
   123→// here for compatibility. In new code, use instances.RepetitionData instead.
   124→type InstanceKeyEvalData = instances.RepetitionData
   125→
   126→// EvalDataForInstanceKey constructs a suitable InstanceKeyEvalData for
   127→// evaluating in a context that has the given instance key.
   128→//
   129→// The forEachMap argument can be nil when preparing for evaluation
   130→// in a context where each.value is prohibited, such as a destroy-time
   131→// provisioner. In that case, the returned EachValue will always be
   132→// cty.NilVal.
   133→func EvalDataForInstanceKey(key addrs.InstanceKey, forEachMap map[string]cty.Value) InstanceKeyEvalData {
   134→	var evalData InstanceKeyEvalData
   135→	if key == nil {
   136→		return evalData
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	keyValue := key.Value()
   140→	switch keyValue.Type() {
   141→	case cty.String:
   142→		evalData.EachKey = keyValue
   143→		evalData.EachValue = forEachMap[keyValue.AsString()]
   144→	case cty.Number:
   145→		evalData.CountIndex = keyValue
   146→	}
   147→	return evalData
   148→}
   149→
   150→// EvalDataForNoInstanceKey is a value of InstanceKeyData that sets no instance
   151→// key values at all, suitable for use in contexts where no keyed instance
   152→// is relevant.
   153→var EvalDataForNoInstanceKey = InstanceKeyEvalData{}
   154→
   155→// evaluationStateData must implement lang.Data
   156→var _ lang.Data = (*evaluationStateData)(nil)
   157→
   158→// StaticValidateReferences calls [Evaluator.StaticValidateReferences] on
   159→// the evaluator embedded in this data object, using this data object's
   160→// static module path.
   161→func (d *evaluationStateData) StaticValidateReferences(refs []*addrs.Reference, self addrs.Referenceable, source addrs.Referenceable) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   162→	return d.Evaluator.StaticValidateReferences(refs, d.ModulePath.Module(), self, source)
   163→}
   164→
   165→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetCountAttr(addr addrs.CountAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   166→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   167→	switch addr.Name {
   168→
   169→	case "index":
   170→		idxVal := d.InstanceKeyData.CountIndex
   171→		if idxVal == cty.NilVal {
   172→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   173→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   174→				Summary:  `Reference to "count" in non-counted context`,
   175→				Detail:   `The "count" object can only be used in "module", "resource", and "data" blocks, and only when the "count" argument is set.`,
   176→				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   177→			})
   178→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.Number), diags
   179→		}
   180→		return idxVal, diags
   181→
   182→	default:
   183→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   184→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   185→			Summary:  `Invalid "count" attribute`,
   186→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "count" object does not have an attribute named %q. The only supported attribute is count.index, which is the index of each instance of a resource block that has the "count" argument set.`, addr.Name),
   187→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   188→		})
   189→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   190→	}
   191→}
   192→
   193→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetForEachAttr(addr addrs.ForEachAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   194→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   195→	var returnVal cty.Value
   196→	switch addr.Name {
   197→
   198→	case "key":
   199→		returnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachKey
   200→	case "value":
   201→		returnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachValue
   202→
   203→		if returnVal == cty.NilVal {
   204→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   205→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   206→				Summary:  `each.value cannot be used in this context`,
   207→				Detail:   `A reference to "each.value" has been used in a context in which it is unavailable, such as when the configuration no longer contains the value in its "for_each" expression. Remove this reference to each.value in your configuration to work around this error.`,
   208→				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   209→			})
   210→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags
   211→		}
   212→	default:
   213→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   214→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   215→			Summary:  `Invalid "each" attribute`,
   216→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "each" object does not have an attribute named %q. The supported attributes are each.key and each.value, the current key and value pair of the "for_each" attribute set.`, addr.Name),
   217→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   218→		})
   219→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   220→	}
   221→
   222→	if returnVal == cty.NilVal {
   223→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   224→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   225→			Summary:  `Reference to "each" in context without for_each`,
   226→			Detail:   `The "each" object can be used only in "module" or "resource" blocks, and only when the "for_each" argument is set.`,
   227→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   228→		})
   229→		return cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags
   230→	}
   231→	return returnVal, diags
   232→}
   233→
   234→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetInputVariable(addr addrs.InputVariable, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   235→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   236→
   237→	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   238→	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   239→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   240→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   241→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   242→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   243→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("input variable read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   244→	}
   245→
   246→	config := moduleConfig.Module.Variables[addr.Name]
   247→	if config != nil {
   248→		var suggestions []string
   249→		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Variables {
   250→			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   251→		}
   252→		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   253→		if suggestion != "" {
   254→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   255→		} else {
   256→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" This variable can be declared with a variable %q {} block.", addr.Name)
   257→		}
   258→
   259→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   260→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   261→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared input variable`,
   262→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An input variable with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   263→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   264→		})
   265→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   266→	}
   267→
   268→	// During the validate walk, input variables are always unknown so
   269→	// that we are validating the configuration for all possible input values
   270→	// rather than for a specific set. Checking against a specific set of
   271→	// input values then happens during the plan walk.
   272→	//
   273→	// This is important because otherwise the validation walk will tend to be
   274→	// overly strict, requiring expressions throughout the configuration to
   275→	// be complicated to accommodate all possible inputs, whereas returning
   276→	// unknown here allows for simpler patterns like using input values as
   277→	// guards to broadly enable/disable resources, avoid processing things
   278→	// that are disabled, etc. Terraform's static validation leans towards
   279→	// being liberal in what it accepts because the subsequent plan walk has
   280→	// more information available and so can be more conservative.
   281→	if d.Operation != walkValidate {
   282→		// We should still capture the statically-configured marks during
   283→		// the validate walk.
   284→		ret := cty.UnknownVal(config.Type)
   285→		if config.Sensitive {
   286→			ret = ret.Mark(marks.Sensitive)
   287→		}
   288→		if config.Ephemeral {
   289→			ret = ret.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   290→		}
   291→		return ret, diags
   292→	}
   293→
   294→	val := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetInputVariableValue(d.ModulePath.InputVariable(addr.Name))
   295→
   296→	// Mark if sensitive and/or ephemeral
   297→	if config.Sensitive {
   298→		val = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)
   299→	}
   300→	if config.Ephemeral {
   301→		val = val.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   302→	}
   303→
   304→	return val, diags
   305→}
   306→
   307→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetLocalValue(addr addrs.LocalValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   308→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   309→
   310→	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   311→	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   312→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   313→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   314→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   315→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   316→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("local value read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   317→	}
   318→
   319→	config := moduleConfig.Module.Locals[addr.Name]
   320→	if config == nil {
   321→		var suggestions []string
   322→		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Locals {
   323→			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   324→		}
   325→		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   326→		if suggestion != "" {
   327→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   328→		}
   329→
   330→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   331→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   332→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared local value`,
   333→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A local value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   334→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   335→		})
   336→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   337→	}
   338→
   339→	val := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetLocalValue(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   340→	return val, diags
   341→}
   342→
   343→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetModule(addr addrs.ModuleCall, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   344→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   345→
   346→	// Output results live in the module that declares them, which is one of
   347→	// the child module instances of our current module path.
   348→	moduleAddr := d.ModulePath.Module().Child(addr.Name)
   349→	absAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   350→
   351→	parentCfg := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   352→	callConfig, ok := parentCfg.Module.ModuleCalls[addr.Name]
   353→	if !ok {
   354→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   355→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   356→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared module`,
   357→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The configuration contains no %s.`, moduleAddr),
   358→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   359→		})
   360→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   361→	}
   362→
   363→	// We'll consult the configuration to see what output names we are
   364→	// expecting, so we can ensure the resulting object is of the expected
   365→	// type even if our data is incomplete for some reason.
   366→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.Descendant(moduleAddr)
   367→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   368→		// should never happen, since we have a valid module call above, this
   369→		// should be caught during static validation.
   370→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("output value read from %s, which has no configuration", moduleAddr))
   371→	}
   372→	outputConfigs := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs
   373→
   374→	// We don't do instance expansion during validation, and so we need to
   375→	// return an unknown value. Technically we should always return
   376→	// cty.DynamicVal here because the final value during plan will always
   377→	// be an object or tuple type with unpredictable attributes/elements,
   378→	// but because we never actually carry values forward from validation to
   379→	// planning we lie a little here and return unknown list and map types,
   380→	// just to give us more opportunities to catch author mistakes during
   381→	// validation.
   382→	//
   383→	// This means that in practice any expression that refers to a module
   384→	// call must be written to be valid for either a collection type or
   385→	// structural type of similar kind, so that it can be considered as
   386→	// valid during both the validate and plan walks.
   387→	if d.Operation == walkValidate {
   388→		atys := make(map[string]cty.Type, len(outputConfigs))
   389→		for name := range outputConfigs {
   390→			atys[name] = cty.DynamicPseudoType // output values are dynamically-typed
   391→		}
   392→		instTy := cty.Object(atys)
   393→
   394→		switch {
   395→		case callConfig.Count != nil:
   396→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.List(instTy)), diags
   397→		case callConfig.ForEach != nil:
   398→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.Map(instTy)), diags
   399→		default:
   400→			return cty.UnknownVal(instTy), diags
   401→		}
   402→	}
   403→
   404→	// For all other walk types, we proceed to dynamic evaluation of individual
   405→	// instances, using the global instance expander. An earlier graph node
   406→	// should always have registered the expansion of this module call before
   407→	// we get here, unless there's a bug in the graph builders.
   408→	allInstances := d.Evaluator.Instances
   409→	instKeyType, instKeys, known := allInstances.ExpandAbsModuleCall(absAddr)
   410→	if !known {
   411→		// If we don't know which instances exist then we can't really predict
   412→		// anything at all. We can't even predict the return type based on
   413→		// instKeyType because output values are dynamically-typed and so
   414→		// our final result will always be an object or tuple type whose
   415→		// attribute/element count we cannot predict.
   416→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   417→	}
   418→
   419→	instanceObjVal := func(instKey addrs.InstanceKey) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   420→		// This function must always return a valid value, even if it's
   421→		// just a cty.DynamicVal placeholder accompanying error diagnostics.
   422→		var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   423→
   424→		namedVals := d.Evaluator.NamedValues
   425→		moduleInstAddr := absAddr.Instance(instKey)
   426→		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(outputConfigs))
   427→		for name := range outputConfigs {
   428→			outputAddr := moduleInstAddr.OutputValue(name)
   429→
   430→			// Although we do typically expect the graph dependencies to
   431→			// ensure that values get registered before they are needed,
   432→			// we track depedencies with specific output values where
   433→			// possible, instead of with entire module calls, and so
   434→			// in this specific case it's valid for some of this call's
   435→			// output values to not be known yet, with the graph builder
   436→			// being responsible for making sure that no expression
   437→			// in the configuration can actually observe that.
   438→			if !namedVals.HasOutputValue(outputAddr) {
   439→				attrs[name] = cty.DynamicVal
   440→				continue
   441→			}
   442→			outputVal := namedVals.GetOutputValue(outputAddr)
   443→			attrs[name] = outputVal
   444→		}
   445→
   446→		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   447→	}
   448→
   449→	switch instKeyType {
   450→
   451→	case addrs.NoKeyType:
   452→		// In this case we should always have exactly one instance that
   453→		// is addrs.NoKey. If not then there's a bug in the [instances.Expander]
   454→		// implementation.
   455→		if len(instKeys) != 1 {
   456→			panic(fmt.Sprintf("module call has no instance key type but has %d instances (should be 1)", len(instKeys)))
   457→		}
   458→		ret, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKeys[0])
   459→		diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   460→		return ret, diags
   461→
   462→	case addrs.IntKeyType:
   463→		// We can assume that the instance keys are in ascending numerical order
   464→		// and are consecutive, per the contract of allInstances.ExpandModuleCall.
   465→		elems := make([]cty.Value, 0, len(instKeys))
   466→		for _, instKey := range instKeys {
   467→			instVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)
   468→			elems = append(elems, instVal)
   469→			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   470→		}
   471→		return cty.TupleVal(elems), diags
   472→
   473→	case addrs.StringKeyType:
   474→		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(instKeys))
   475→		for _, instKey := range instKeys {
   476→			instVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)
   477→			attrs[string(instKey.(addrs.StringKey))] = instVal
   478→			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   479→		}
   480→		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   481→
   482→	default:
   483→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   484→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   485→			Summary:  `Unsupported instance key type`,
   486→			Detail: fmt.Sprintf(
   487→				`Module call %s has instance key type %#v, which is not supported by the expression evaluator. This is a bug in Terraform.`,
   488→				absAddr, instKeyType,
   489→			),
   490→			Subject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   491→		})
   492→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   493→	}
   494→}
   495→
   496→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   497→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   498→	// First we'll consult the configuration to see if an resource of this
   499→	// name is declared at all.
   500→	moduleAddr := d.ModulePath
   501→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(moduleAddr)
   502→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   503→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   504→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   505→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("resource value read from %s, which has no configuration", moduleAddr))
   506→	}
   507→
   508→	config := moduleConfig.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr)
   509→	if config == nil {
   510→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   511→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   512→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared resource`,
   513→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A resource %q %q has not been declared in %s`, addr.Type, addr.Name, moduleDisplayAddr(moduleAddr)),
   514→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   515→		})
   516→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   517→	}
   518→
   519→	// Much of this function was written before we had factored out the handling
   520→	// of instance keys into the separate instance expander model, and so it
   521→	// does a bunch of instance-related work itself below.
   522→	//
   523→	// Currently, unknown instance keys are only possible when planning with
   524→	// DeferralAllowed set to true in the PlanOpts, which should only be the
   525→	// case in the stacks runtime (not the "normal terraform" modules runtime).
   526→	// Thus, we have some amount of duplicated code remaining, to be more
   527→	// certain that stacks-specific behaviors won't leak out into the standard
   528→	// runtime.
   529→	//
   530→	// TODO: When deferred actions are more stable and robust in stacks, it
   531→	// would be nice to rework this function to rely on the ResourceInstanceKeys
   532→	// result for _all_ of its work, rather than continuing to duplicate a bunch
   533→	// of the logic we've tried to encapsulate over ther already.
   534→	if d.Operation == walkPlan || d.Operation == walkApply {
   535→		if _, _, hasUnknownKeys := d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys(addr.Absolute(moduleAddr)); !hasUnknownKeys {
   536→			// There really isn't anything interesting we can do in this situation,
   537→			// because it means we have an unknown for_each/count, in which case
   538→			// we can't even predict what the result type will be because it
   539→			// would be either an object or tuple type decided based on the instance
   540→			// keys.
   541→			// (We can't get in here for a single-instance resource because in that
   542→			// case we would know that there's only one key and it's addrs.NoKey,
   543→			// so we'll fall through to the other logic below.)
   544→			unknownVal := cty.DynamicVal
   545→
   546→			// If an ephemeral resource is deferred we need to mark the returned unknown value as ephemeral
   547→			if addr.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {
   548→				unknownVal = unknownVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   549→			}
   550→			return unknownVal, diags
   551→		}
   552→	}
   553→
   554→	// Build the provider address from configuration, since we may not have
   555→	// state available in all cases.
   556→	// We need to build an abs provider address, but we can use a default
   557→	// instance since we're only interested in the schema.
   558→	schema := d.getResourceSchema(addr, config.Provider)
   559→	if schema == nil {
   560→		// This shouldn't happen, since validation before we get here should've
   561→		// taken care of it, but we'll show a reasonable error message anyway.
   562→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   563→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   564→			Summary:  `Missing resource type schema`,
   565→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("No schema is available for %s in %s. This is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.", addr, config.Provider),
   566→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   567→		})
   568→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   569→	}
   570→	ty := schema.ImpliedType()
   571→
   572→	if addr.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {
   573→		// FIXME: This does not yet work with deferrals, and it would be nice to
   574→		// find some way to refactor this so that the following code is not so
   575→		// tethered to the current implementation details. Instead we should
   576→		// have an abstract idea of first determining what instances the
   577→		// resource has (using d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys) and
   578→		// then retrieving the value for each instance to assemble into the
   579→		// result, using some per-resource-mode logic maintained elsewhere.
   580→		return d.getEphemeralResource(addr, rng)
   581→	}
   582→
   583→	rs := d.Evaluator.State.Resource(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   584→
   585→	if rs == nil {
   586→		switch d.Operation {
   587→		case walkPlan, walkApply:
   588→			// During plan and apply as we evaluate each removed instance they
   589→			// are removed from the working state. Since we know there are no
   590→			// instances, return an empty container of the expected type.
   591→			switch {
   592→			case config.Count != nil:
   593→				return cty.EmptyTupleVal, diags
   594→			case config.ForEach != nil:
   595→				return cty.EmptyObjectVal, diags
   596→			default:
   597→				// While we can reference an expanded resource with 0
   598→				// instances, we cannot reference instances that do not exist.
   599→				// Due to the fact that we may have direct references to
   600→				// instances that may end up in a root output during destroy
   601→				// (since a planned destroy cannot yet remove root outputs), we
   602→				// need to return a dynamic value here to allow evaluation to
   603→				// continue.
   604→				log.Printf("[ERROR] unknown instance %q referenced during %s", addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath), d.Operation)
   605→				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   606→			}
   607→
   608→		case walkImport:
   609→			// Import does not yet plan resource changes, so new resources from
   610→			// config are not going to be found here. Once walkImport fully
   611→			// plans resources, this case should not longer be needed.
   612→			// In the single instance case, we can return a typed unknown value
   613→			// for the instance to better satisfy other expressions using the
   614→			// value. This of course will not help if statically known
   615→			// attributes are expected to be known elsewhere, but reduces the
   616→			// number of problematic configs for now.
   617→			// Unlike in plan and apply above we can't be sure the count or
   618→			// for_each instances are empty, so we return a DynamicVal. We
   619→			// don't really have a good value to return otherwise -- empty
   620→			// values will fail for direct index expressions, and unknown
   621→			// Lists and Maps could fail in some type unifications.
   622→			switch {
   623→			case config.Count != nil:
   624→				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   625→			case config.ForEach != nil:
   626→				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   627→			default:
   628→				return cty.UnknownVal(ty), diags
   629→			}
   630→
   631→		default:
   632→			// We should only end up here during the validate walk (or
   633→			// console/eval), since later walks should have at least partial
   634→			// states populated for all resources in the configuration.
   635→			return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   636→		}
   637→	}
   638→
   639→	// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource
   640→	// or resources that are in the state.
   641→	instances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}
   642→
   643→	// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the
   644→	// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be
   645→	// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.
   646→	for key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {
   647→		instances[key] = value
   648→	}
   649→
   650→	// Decode all instances in the current state
   651→	pendingDestroy := d.Operation == walkDestroy
   652→	for key, is := range rs.Instances {
   653→		if _, ok := instances[key]; ok {
   654→			// Then we've already loaded this instance from the deferrals so
   655→			// we'll just ignore it being in state.
   656→			continue
   657→		}
   658→		// Otherwise, we'll load the instance from state.
   659→
   660→		if is == nil || is.Current == nil {
   661→			// Assume we're dealing with an instance that hasn't been created yet.
   662→			instances[key] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   663→			continue
   664→		}
   665→
   666→		instAddr := addr.Instance(key).Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   667→		change := d.Evaluator.Changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(instAddr, addrs.NotDeposed)
   668→		if change != nil {
   669→			// Don't take any resources that are yet to be deleted into account.
   670→			// If the referenced resource is CreateBeforeDestroy, then orphaned
   671→			// instances will be in the state, as they are not destroyed until
   672→			// after their dependants are updated.
   673→			if change.Action == plans.Delete {
   674→				if !pendingDestroy {
   675→					continue
   676→				}
   677→			}
   678→		}
   679→
   680→		// Planned resources are temporarily stored in state with empty values,
   681→		// and need to be replaced by the planned value here.
   682→		if is.Current.Status == states.ObjectPlanned {
   683→			if change == nil {
   684→				// FIXME: This is usually an unfortunate case where we need to
   685→				// lookup an individual instance referenced via "self" for
   686→				// postconditions which we know exists, but because evaluation
   687→				// must always get the resource in aggregate some instance
   688→				// changes may not yet be registered.
   689→				instances[key] = cty.DynamicVal
   690→				// log the problem for debugging, since it may be a legitimate error we can't catch
   691→				log.Printf("[WARN] instance %s is marked as having a change pending but that change is not recorded in the plan", instAddr)
   692→				continue
   693→			}
   694→			instances[key] = change.After
   695→			continue
   696→		}
   697→
   698→		ios, err := is.Current.Decode(ty)
   699→		if err != nil {
   700→			// This shouldn't happen, since by the time we get here we
   701→			// should have upgraded the state data already.
   702→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   703→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   704→				Summary:  "Invalid resource instance data in state",
   705→				Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("Instance %s data could not be decoded from the state: %s.", instAddr, err),
   706→				Subject:  &config.DeclRange,
   707→			})
   708→			continue
   709→		}
   710→
   711→		val := ios.Value
   712→
   713→		instances[key] = val
   714→	}
   715→
   716→	// ret should be populated with a valid value in all cases below
   717→	var ret cty.Value
   718→
   719→	switch {
   720→	case config.Count != nil:
   721→		// figure out what the last index we have is
   722→		length := -1
   723→		for key := range instances {
   724→			intKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)
   725→			if !ok {
   726→				continue
   727→			}
   728→			if int(intKey) >= length {
   729→				length = int(intKey) + 1
   730→			}
   731→		}
   732→
   733→		if length > 0 {
   734→			vals := make([]cty.Value, length)
   735→			for key, instance := range instances {
   736→				intKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)
   737→				if !ok {
   738→					// old key from state, which isn't valid for evaluation
   739→					continue
   740→				}
   741→
   742→				vals[int(intKey)] = instance
   743→			}
   744→
   745→			// Insert unknown values where there are any missing instances
   746→			for i, v := range vals {
   747→				if v == cty.NilVal {
   748→					vals[i] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   749→				}
   750→			}
   751→			ret = cty.TupleVal(vals)
   752→		} else {
   753→			ret = cty.EmptyTupleVal
   754→		}
   755→
   756→	case config.ForEach != nil:
   757→		vals := make(map[string]cty.Value)
   758→		for key, instance := range instances {
   759→			strKey, ok := key.(addrs.StringKey)
   760→			if !ok {
   761→				// old key that is being dropped and not used for evaluation
   762→				continue
   763→			}
   764→			vals[string(strKey)] = instance
   765→		}
   766→
   767→		if len(vals) > 0 {
   768→			// We use an object rather than a map here because resource schemas
   769→			// may include dynamically-typed attributes, which will then cause
   770→			// each instance to potentially have a different runtime type even
   771→			// though they all conform to the static schema.
   772→			ret = cty.ObjectVal(vals)
   773→		} else {
   774→			ret = cty.EmptyObjectVal
   775→		}
   776→
   777→	default:
   778→		val, ok := instances[addrs.NoKey]
   779→		if !ok {
   780→			// if the instance is missing, insert an unknown value
   781→			val = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   782→		}
   783→
   784→		ret = val
   785→	}
   786→
   787→	return ret, diags
   788→}
   789→
   790→func (d *evaluationStateData) getEphemeralResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   791→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   792→
   793→	if d.Operation == walkValidate || d.Operation == walkEval {
   794→		// Ephemeral instances are never live during the validate walk. Eval is
   795→		// similarly offline, and since there is no value stored we can't return
   796→		// anything other than dynamic.
   797→		return cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags
   798→	}
   799→
   800→	// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource
   801→	// or resources that are in the state.
   802→	instances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}
   803→
   804→	// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the
   805→	// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be
   806→	// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.
   807→	for key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {
   808→		instances[key] = value
   809→	}
   810→
   811→	absAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   812→	keyType, keys, haveUnknownKeys := d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys(absAddr)
   813→	if haveUnknownKeys {
   814→		// We can probably do better than totally unknown at least for a
   815→		// single-instance resource, but we'll just keep it simple for now.
   816→		// Result must be marked as ephemeral so that we can still catch
   817→		// attempts to use the results in non-ephemeral locations, so that
   818→		// the operator doesn't end up trapped with an error on a subsequent
   819→		// plan/apply round.
   820→		return cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags
   821→	}
   822→
   823→	ephems := d.Evaluator.EphemeralResources
   824→	getInstValue := func(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   825→		var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   826→
   827→		// If we have a deferred instance with this key we don't need to check if it is live or not,
   828→		// it has not been created so we can just return the deferred value.
   829→		if v, ok := instances[addr.Resource.Key]; ok {
   830→			return v, diags
   831→		}
   832→
   833→		val, isLive := ephems.InstanceValue(addr)
   834→		if !isLive {
   835→			// If the instance is no longer "live" by the time we're accessing
   836→			// it then that suggests that it needed renewal and renewal has
   837→			// failed, and so the object's value is no longer usable. We'll
   838→			// still return the value in case it's somehow useful for diagnosis,
   839→			// but we return an error to prevent further evaluation of whatever
   840→			// other expression depended on the liveness of this object.
   841→			//
   842→			// This error message is written on the assumption that it will
   843→			// always appear alongside the provider's renewal error, but that'll
   844→			// be exposed only once the (now-zombied) ephemeral resource is
   845→			// eventually closed, so that we can avoid returning the same error
   846→			// multiple times.
   847→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   848→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   849→				Summary:  "Ephemeral resource instance has expired",
   850→				Detail: fmt.Sprintf(
   851→					"The remote object for %s is no longer available due to a renewal error, so Terraform cannot evaluate this expression.",
   852→					addr,
   853→				),
   854→				Subject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   855→			})
   856→		}
   857→		if val == cty.NilVal {
   858→			val = cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   859→		}
   860→		return val, diags
   861→	}
   862→
   863→	switch keyType {
   864→	case addrs.NoKeyType:
   865→		// For "no key" we're returning just a single object representing
   866→		// the single instance of this resource.
   867→		instVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(absAddr.Instance(addrs.NoKey))
   868→		diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   869→		return instVal, diags
   870→	case addrs.IntKeyType:
   871→		// For integer keys we're returning a tuple-typed value whose
   872→		// indices are the keys.
   873→		elems := make([]cty.Value, len(keys))
   874→		for _, key := range keys {
   875→			idx := int(key.(addrs.IntKey))
   876→			instAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)
   877→			instVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)
   878→			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   879→			elems[idx] = instVal
   880→		}
   881→		return cty.TupleVal(elems), diags
   882→	case addrs.StringKeyType:
   883→		// For string keys we're returning an object-typed value whose
   884→		// attributes are the keys.
   885→		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(keys))
   886→		for _, key := range keys {
   887→			attrName := string(key.(addrs.StringKey))
   888→			instAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)
   889→			instVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)
   890→			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   891→			attrs[attrName] = instVal
   892→		}
   893→		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   894→	default:
   895→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unhandled instance key type %#v", keyType))
   896→	}
   897→}
   898→
   899→func (d *evaluationStateData) getResourceSchema(addr addrs.Resource, providerAddr addrs.Provider) *configschema.Block {
   900→	schema, _, err := d.Evaluator.Plugins.ResourceTypeSchema(providerAddr, addr.Mode, addr.Type)
   901→	if err != nil {
   902→		// We have plently other codepaths that will detect and report
   903→		// schema lookup errors before we'd reach this point, so we'll just
   904→		// treat a failure here the same as having no schema.
   905→		return nil
   906→	}
   907→	return schema
   908→}
   909→
   910→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetOutput(addr addrs.OutputValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   911→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   912→
   913→	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   914→	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   915→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   916→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   917→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   918→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   919→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("output value read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   920→	}
   921→
   922→	config := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs[addr.Name]
   923→	if config == nil {
   924→		var suggestions []string
   925→		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Outputs {
   926→			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   927→		}
   928→		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   929→		if suggestion != "" {
   930→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   931→		}
   932→
   933→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   934→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   935→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared output value`,
   936→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An output value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   937→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   938→		})
   939→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   940→	}
   941→
   942→	output := d.Evaluator.State.OutputValue(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   943→	if output == nil {
   944→		// Then the output itself returned null, so we'll package that up and
   945→		// pass it on.
   946→		output = &states.OutputValue{
   947→			Addr:      addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath),
   948→			Value:     cty.NilVal,
   949→			Sensitive: config.Sensitive,
   950→		}
   951→	} else if output.Value == cty.NilVal || output.Value.IsNull() {
   952→		// Then we did get a value but Terraform itself thought it was NilVal
   953→		// so we treat this as if the value isn't yet known.
   954→		output.Value = cty.DynamicVal
   955→	}
   956→
   957→	val := output.Value
   958→	if output.Sensitive {
   959→		val = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)
   960→	}
   961→
   962→	return val, diags
   963→}
   964→
   965→// moduleDisplayAddr returns a string describing the given module instance
   966→// address that is appropriate for returning to users in situations where the
   967→// root module is possible. Specifically, it returns "the root module" if the
   968→// root module instance is given, or a string representation of the module
   969→// address otherwise.
   970→func moduleDisplayAddr(addr addrs.ModuleInstance) string {
   971→	switch {
   972→	case addr.IsRoot():
   973→		return "the root module"
   974→	default:
   975→		return addr.String()
   976→	}
   977→}

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/evaluate.go", "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/didyoumean\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/namedvals\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/resources/ephemeral\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// Evaluator provides the necessary contextual data for evaluating expressions\n// for a particular walk operation.\ntype Evaluator struct {\n\t// Operation defines what type of operation this evaluator is being used\n\t// for.\n\tOperation walkOperation\n\n\t// Meta is contextual metadata about the current operation.\n\tMeta *ContextMeta\n\n\t// Config is the root node in the configuration tree.\n\tConfig *configs.Config\n\n\t// Instances tracks the dynamic instances that are associated with each\n\t// module call or resource. Otherwise, it behaves as an alias for the given\n// address.\nfunc (e *Evaluator) Scope(data lang.Data, self addrs.Referenceable, source addrs.Referenceable, extFuncs lang.ExternalFuncs) *lang.Scope {\n\treturn &lang.Scope{\n\t\tData:          data,\n\t\tParseRef:      addrs.ParseRef,\n\t\tSelfAddr:      self,\n\t\tSourceAddr:    source,\n\t\tPureOnly:      e.Operation != walkApply && e.Operation != walkDestroy && e.Operation != walkEval,\n\t\tBaseDir:       \".\", // Always current working directory for now.\n\t\tPlanTimestamp: e.PlanTimestamp,\n\t\tExternalFuncs: extFuncs,\n\t}\n}\n\n// evaluationStateData is an implementation of lang.Data that resolves\n// references primarily (but not exclusively) using information from a State.\ntype evaluationStateData struct {\n\t*evaluationData\n\n\t// ModulePath is the path through the dynamic module tree to the module\n\t// that references will be resolved relative to.\n\tModulePath addrs.ModuleInstance\n\n\t// InstanceKeyData describes the values, if any, that are accessible due\n\t// to repetition of a containing object using \"count\" or \"for_each\"\n\t// arguments. In new code, use instances.RepetitionData instead.\ntype InstanceKeyEvalData = instances.RepetitionData\n\n// EvalDataForInstanceKey constructs a suitable InstanceKeyEvalData for\n// evaluating in a context that has the given instance key.\n//\n// The forEachMap argument can be nil when preparing for evaluation\n// in a context where each.value is prohibited, such as a destroy-time\n// provisioner. forEachMap[keyValue.AsString()]\n\tcase cty.Number:\n\t\tevalData.CountIndex = keyValue\n\t}\n\treturn evalData\n}\n\n// EvalDataForNoInstanceKey is a value of InstanceKeyData that sets no fmt.Sprintf(`The \"count\" object does not have an attribute named %q. The only supported attribute is count.index, which is the index of each instance of a resource block that has the \"count\" argument set.`, addr.Name),\n\t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (d *evaluationStateData) GetForEachAttr(addr addrs.ForEachAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar returnVal cty.Value\n\tswitch addr.Name {\n\n\tcase \"key\":\n\t\treturnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachKey\n\tcase \"value\":\n\t\treturnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachValue\n\n\t\tif returnVal == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\t\tSummary:  `each.value cannot be used in this context`,\n\t\t\t\tDetail:   `A reference to \"each.value\" has been used in a context in which it is unavailable, such as when the configuration no longer contains the value in its \"for_each\" expression. The supported attributes are each.key and each.value, the current key and value pair of the \"for_each\" attribute set.`, addr.Name),\n\t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t}\n\n\tif returnVal == cty.NilVal {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to \"each\" in context without for_each`,\n\t\t\tDetail:   `The \"each\" object can be used only in \"module\" or \"resource\" blocks, and only when the \"for_each\" argument is set.`,\n\t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags\n\t}\n\treturn returnVal, diags\n}\n\nfunc (d *evaluationStateData) GetInputVariable(addr addrs.InputVariable, rng message if not.\n\tmoduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)\n\tif moduleConfig == nil {\n\t\t// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module\n\t\t// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"input variable read from %s, which has no configuration\", d.ModulePath))\n\t}\n\n\tconfig := moduleConfig.Module.Variables[addr.Name]\n\tif config != nil {\n\t\tvar suggestions []string\n\t\tfor k := range moduleConfig.Module.Variables {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions, k)\n\t\t}\n\t\tsuggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)\n\t\tif suggestion != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsuggestion = fmt.Sprintf(\" Did you mean %q?\", suggestion)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tsuggestion = fmt.Sprintf(\" This variable can be declared with a variable %q {} block.\", addr.Name)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to undeclared input diags\n\t}\n\n\t// During the validate walk, input variables are always unknown so\n\t// that we are validating the configuration for all possible input values\n\t// rather than for a specific set. requiring expressions throughout the configuration to\n\t// be complicated to accommodate all possible inputs, whereas returning\n\t// unknown here allows for simpler patterns like using input values ret.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ret, diags\n\t}\n\n\tval := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetInputVariableValue(d.ModulePath.InputVariable(addr.Name))\n\n\t// Mark if sensitive and/or ephemeral\n\tif config.Sensitive {\n\t\tval = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)\n\t}\n\tif config.Ephemeral {\n\t\tval = val.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\n\t}\n\n\treturn val, diags\n}\n\nfunc (d fmt.Sprintf(`The configuration contains no %s.`, moduleAddr),\n\t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// We'll consult the configuration to see what Build the provider address from configuration, since we may not have\n\t// state available in all cases.\n\t// We need to build an abs provider address, but we can use a default\n\t// instance since we're only interested in the schema.\n\tschema := d.getResourceSchema(addr, config.Provider)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// This shouldn't happen, since validation before we get here should've\n\t\t// taken care of it, but we'll show a reasonable error message anyway.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  `Missing resource type schema`,\n\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"No schema is available for %s in %s. This is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.\", addr, config.Provider),\n\t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t}\n\tty := schema.ImpliedType()\n\n\tif addr.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// FIXME: This does not yet work with deferrals, and it would be nice to\n\t\t// find some way to refactor this so that the following code is not so\n\t\t// tethered to the current implementation details. Instead we should\n\t\t// have an abstract idea of first determining what instances the\n\t\t// resource has (using d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys) and\n\t\t// then retrieving the value for each instance to assemble into the\n\t\t// result, using some per-resource-mode logic maintained elsewhere.\n\t\treturn d.getEphemeralResource(addr, rng)\n\t}\n\n\trs := d.Evaluator.State.Resource(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))\n\n\tif rs == nil {\n\t\tswitch d.Operation {\n\t\tcase walkPlan, walkApply:\n\t\t\t// During plan and apply as we evaluate each removed instance they\n\t\t\t// are removed from the working state. Since we know there are no\n\t\t\t// instances, return an empty container of the expected type.\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase config.Count != nil:\n\t\t\t\treturn cty.EmptyTupleVal, diags\n\t\t\tcase config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\t\t\treturn cty.EmptyObjectVal, diags\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t// While we can reference an expanded resource with 0\n\t\t\t\t// instances, we cannot reference instances that do not exist.\n\t\t\t\t// Due to the fact that we may have direct references to\n\t\t\t\t// instances that may end up in a root output during destroy\n\t\t\t\t// (since a planned destroy cannot yet remove root outputs), we\n\t\t\t\t// need to return a dynamic value here to allow evaluation to\n\t\t\t\t// continue.\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] unknown instance %q referenced during %s\", addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath), d.Operation)\n\t\t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase walkImport:\n\t\t\t// Import does not yet plan resource changes, so new resources from\n\t\t\t// config are not going to be found here. Once walkImport fully\n\t\t\t// plans resources, this case should not longer be needed.\n\t\t\t// In the single instance case, we can return a typed unknown value\n\t\t\t// for the instance to better satisfy other expressions using the\n\t\t\t// value. This of course will not help if statically known\n\t\t\t// attributes are expected to be known elsewhere, but reduces the\n\t\t\t// number of problematic configs for now.\n\t\t\t// Unlike in plan and apply above we can't be sure the count or\n\t\t\t// for_each instances are empty, so we return a DynamicVal. We\n\t\t\t// don't really have a good value to return otherwise -- empty\n\t\t\t// values will fail for direct index expressions, and unknown\n\t\t\t// Lists and Maps could fail in some type unifications.\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase config.Count != nil:\n\t\t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t\t\tcase config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(ty), diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// We should only end up here during the validate walk (or\n\t\t\t// console/eval), since later walks should have at least partial\n\t\t\t// states populated for all resources in the configuration.\n\t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource\n\t// or resources that are in the state.\n\tinstances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}\n\n\t// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the\n\t// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be\n\t// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.\n\tfor key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {\n\t\tinstances[key] = value\n\t}\n\n\t// Decode all instances in the current state\n\tpendingDestroy := d.Operation == walkDestroy\n\tfor key, is := range rs.Instances {\n\t\tif _, ok := instances[key]; ok {\n\t\t\t// Then we've already loaded this instance from the deferrals so\n\t\t\t// we'll just ignore it being in state.\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Otherwise, we'll load the instance from state.\n\n\t\tif is == nil || is.Current == nil {\n\t\t\t// Assume we're dealing with an instance that hasn't been created yet.\n\t\t\tinstances[key] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tinstAddr := addr.Instance(key).Absolute(d.ModulePath)\n\t\tchange := d.Evaluator.Changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(instAddr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\t\tif change != nil {\n\t\t\t// Don't take any resources that are yet to be deleted into account.\n\t\t\t// If the referenced resource is CreateBeforeDestroy, then orphaned\n\t\t\t// instances will be in the state, as they are not destroyed until\n\t\t\t// after their dependants are updated.\n\t\t\tif change.Action == plans.Delete {\n\t\t\t\tif !pendingDestroy {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Planned resources are temporarily stored in state with empty values,\n\t\t// and need to be replaced by the planned value here.\n\t\tif is.Current.Status == states.ObjectPlanned {\n\t\t\tif change == nil {\n\t\t\t\t// FIXME: This is usually an unfortunate case where we need to\n\t\t\t\t// lookup an individual instance referenced via \"self\" for\n\t\t\t\t// postconditions which we know exists, but because evaluation\n\t\t\t\t// must always get the resource in aggregate some instance\n\t\t\t\t// changes may not yet be registered.\n\t\t\t\tinstances[key] = cty.DynamicVal\n\t\t\t\t// log the problem for debugging, since it may be a legitimate error we can't catch\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] instance %s is marked as having a change pending but that change is not recorded in the plan\", instAddr)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tinstances[key] = change.After\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tios, err := is.Current.Decode(ty)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// This shouldn't happen, since by the time we get here we\n\t\t\t// should have upgraded the state data already.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Invalid resource instance data in state\",\n\t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Instance %s data could not be decoded from the state: %s.\", instAddr, err),\n\t\t\t\tSubject:  &config.DeclRange,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tval := ios.Value\n\n\t\tinstances[key] = val\n\t}\n\n\t// ret should be populated with a valid value in all cases below\n\tvar ret cty.Value\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase config.Count != nil:\n\t\t// figure out what the last index we have is\n\t\tlength := -1\n\t\tfor key := range instances {\n\t\t\tintKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)\n\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif int(intKey) >= length {\n\t\t\t\tlength = int(intKey) + 1\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif length > 0 {\n\t\t\tvals := make([]cty.Value, length)\n\t\t\tfor key, instance := range instances {\n\t\t\t\tintKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t// old key from state, which isn't valid for evaluation\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tvals[int(intKey)] = instance\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Insert unknown values where there are any missing instances\n\t\t\tfor i, v := range vals {\n\t\t\t\tif v == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t\t\t\tvals[i] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tret = cty.TupleVal(vals)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tret = cty.EmptyTupleVal\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\tvals := make(map[string]cty.Value)\n\t\tfor key, instance := range instances {\n\t\t\tstrKey, ok := key.(addrs.StringKey)\n\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t// old key that is being dropped and not used for evaluation\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvals[string(strKey)] = instance\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(vals) > 0 {\n\t\t\t// We use an object rather than a map here because resource schemas\n\t\t\t// may include dynamically-typed attributes, which will then cause\n\t\t\t// each instance to potentially have a different runtime type even\n\t\t\t// though they all conform to the static schema.\n\t\t\tret = cty.ObjectVal(vals)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tret = cty.EmptyObjectVal\n\t\t}\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\tval, ok := instances[addrs.NoKey]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// if the instance is missing, insert an unknown value\n\t\t\tval = cty.UnknownVal(ty)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tret = val\n\t}\n\n\treturn ret, diags\n}\n\nfunc (d *evaluationStateData) getEphemeralResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif d.Operation == walkValidate || d.Operation == walkEval {\n\t\t// Ephemeral instances are never live during the validate walk. Eval is\n\t\t// similarly offline, and since there is no value stored we can't return\n\t\t// anything other than dynamic.\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource\n\t// or resources that are in the state.\n\tinstances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}\n\n\t// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the\n\t// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be\n\t// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.\n\tfor key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {\n\t\tinstances[key] = value\n\t}\n\n\tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)\n\tkeyType, keys, haveUnknownKeys := d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys(absAddr)\n\tif haveUnknownKeys {\n\t\t// We can probably do better than totally unknown at least for a\n\t\t// single-instance resource, but we'll just keep it simple for now.\n\t\t// Result must be marked as ephemeral so that we can still catch\n\t\t// attempts to use the results in non-ephemeral locations, so that\n\t\t// the operator doesn't end up trapped with an error on a subsequent\n\t\t// plan/apply round.\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags\n\t}\n\n\tephems := d.Evaluator.EphemeralResources\n\tgetInstValue := func(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t\t// If we have a deferred instance with this key we don't need to check if it is live or not,\n\t\t// it has not been created so we can just return the deferred value.\n\t\tif v, ok := instances[addr.Resource.Key]; ok {\n\t\t\treturn v, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tval, isLive := ephems.InstanceValue(addr)\n\t\tif !isLive {\n\t\t\t// If the instance is no longer \"live\" by the time we're accessing\n\t\t\t// it then that suggests that it needed renewal and renewal has\n\t\t\t// failed, and so the object's value is no longer usable. We'll\n\t\t\t// still return the value in case it's somehow useful for diagnosis,\n\t\t\t// but we return an error to prevent further evaluation of whatever\n\t\t\t// other expression depended on the liveness of this object.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// This error message is written on the assumption that it will\n\t\t\t// always appear alongside the provider's renewal error, but that'll\n\t\t\t// be exposed only once the (now-zombied) ephemeral resource is\n\t\t\t// eventually closed, so that we can avoid returning the same error\n\t\t\t// multiple times.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Ephemeral resource instance has expired\",\n\t\t\t\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"The remote object for %s is no longer available due to a renewal error, so Terraform cannot evaluate this expression.\",\n\t\t\t\t\taddr,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\tSubject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t\tif val == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t\tval = cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn val, diags\n\t}\n\n\tswitch keyType {\n\tcase addrs.NoKeyType:\n\t\t// For \"no key\" we're returning just a single object representing\n\t\t// the single instance of this resource.\n\t\tinstVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(absAddr.Instance(addrs.NoKey))\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\t\treturn instVal, diags\n\tcase addrs.IntKeyType:\n\t\t// For integer keys we're returning a tuple-typed value whose\n\t\t// indices are the keys.\n\t\telems := make([]cty.Value, len(keys))\n\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\t\tidx := int(key.(addrs.IntKey))\n\t\t\tinstAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)\n\t\t\tinstVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\t\t\telems[idx] = instVal\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn cty.TupleVal(elems), diags\n\tcase addrs.StringKeyType:\n\t\t// For string keys we're returning an object-typed value whose\n\t\t// attributes are the keys.\n\t\tattrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(keys))\n\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\t\tattrName := string(key.(addrs.StringKey))\n\t\t\tinstAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)\n\t\t\tinstVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\t\t\tattrs[attrName] = instVal\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unhandled instance key type %#v\", keyType))\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (d *evaluationStateData) getResourceSchema(addr addrs.Resource, providerAddr addrs.Provider) *configschema.Block {\n\tschema, _, err := d.Evaluator.Plugins.ResourceTypeSchema(providerAddr, addr.Mode, addr.Type)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// We have plently other codepaths that will detect and report\n\t\t// schema lookup errors before we'd reach this point, so we'll just\n\t\t// treat a failure here the same as having no schema.\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn schema\n}\n\nfunc (d *evaluationStateData) GetOutput(addr addrs.OutputValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,\n\t// so we can produce a nice message if not.\n\tmoduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)\n\tif moduleConfig == nil {\n\t\t// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module\n\t\t// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"output value read from %s, which has no configuration\", d.ModulePath))\n\t}\n\n\tconfig := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs[addr.Name]\n\tif config == nil {\n\t\tvar suggestions []string\n\t\tfor k := range moduleConfig.Module.Outputs {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions, k)\n\t\t}\n\t\tsuggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)\n\t\tif suggestion != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsuggestion = fmt.Sprintf(\" Did you mean %q?\", suggestion)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to undeclared output value`,\n\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An output value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),\n\t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t}\n\n\toutput := d.Evaluator.State.OutputValue(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))\n\tif output == nil {\n\t\t// Then the output itself returned null, so we'll package that up and\n\t\t// pass it on.\n\t\toutput = &states.OutputValue{\n\t\t\tAddr:      addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath),\n\t\t\tValue:     cty.NilVal,\n\t\t\tSensitive: config.Sensitive,\n\t\t}\n\t} else if output.Value == cty.NilVal || output.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t// Then we did get a value but Terraform itself thought it was NilVal\n\t\t// so we treat this as if the value isn't yet known.\n\t\toutput.Value = cty.DynamicVal\n\t}\n\n\tval := output.Value\n\tif output.Sensitive {\n\t\tval = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)\n\t}\n\n\treturn val, diags\n}\n\n// moduleDisplayAddr returns a string describing the given module instance\n// address that is appropriate for returning to users in situations where the\n// root module is possible. Specifically, it returns \"the root module\" if the\n// root module instance is given, or a string representation of the module\n// address otherwise.\nfunc moduleDisplayAddr(addr addrs.ModuleInstance) string {\n\tswitch {\n\tcase addr.IsRoot():\n\t\treturn \"the root module\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn addr.String()\n\t}\n}\n", "numLines": 978, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 978}}

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"
    12→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"

    17→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/didyoumean"
    18→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances"
    19→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang"
    20→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks"
    21→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/namedvals"

    24→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/resources/ephemeral"
